headline: GOLD LOGS FOURTH STRAIGHT WEEKLY GAIN, HITS $4,867 — UP 42% IN A YEAR
snippet: Gold climbed to $4,867 per troy ounce on April 17, completing a fourth consecutive weekly gain. The rally follows optimism over US-Iran Strait of Hormuz diplomacy that eased oil prices and geopolitical risk. Spot gold is up 41.73% year-over-year — a return that has crushed every major asset class.

headline: COT REPORT: WHERE SPECULATIVE GOLD AND SILVER MONEY SITS HEADING INTO THE WEEK
snippet: Weekly Commitments of Traders data for gold, silver, and the US Dollar Index as of April 17, 2026. Tracks positioning of commercial hedgers versus large speculators — a key leading indicator for near-term price direction that sophisticated retail investors use to gauge crowding risk.

headline: GOLD SWOT: CHINA IMPORTS 790+ TONNES OF SILVER IN EARLY 2026 — TIGHTEST SUPPLY IN YEARS
snippet: China imported over 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026 — the highest pace in years — driven by surging solar panel production. Combined with the Silver Institute projecting a sixth consecutive annual supply deficit of 67 million ounces, physical silver supply is increasingly strained. Silver has climbed more than 150% over the past year.

headline: GOLD-SILVER RATIO CONTRACTS FROM 105-HANDLE — HISTORICALLY BULLISH SIGNAL FOR SILVER
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in April before turning lower — a historically reliable signal that silver outperforms gold in the weeks ahead. GDX also surged 6.16% in a single session as miners begin to capture the attention of funds that have been sitting on the sidelines.

headline: USA RARE EARTH (USAR) SURGES 7% AS MINE-TO-MAGNET LINE GOES LIVE IN OKLAHOMA
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ: USAR) climbed 7.11% on April 17 after commissioning Phase 1a of its Stillwater, Oklahoma sintered NdFeB magnet production line. The company is targeting 600 metric tons per year of magnet capacity by end of 2026, positioning itself as a second domestic US rare earth supply hub alongside MP Materials. A distribution deal with Arnold Magnetic Technologies adds a downstream channel.

headline: OPINION: WHY A CONTRARIAN WON'T TOUCH USA RARE EARTH DESPITE CRITICAL MINERALS HYPE
snippet: One Motley Fool analyst argues that despite the critical minerals arms race narrative, USA Rare Earth's execution risk, steep valuation, and competition from MP Materials make it too speculative at current prices. A useful counterbalance for retail investors drawn in by the 7% headline rally — two sides to every trade.

headline: NEW YORK COPPER SURGES, LME INDUSTRIAL METALS HIT RECORD — GRASBERG AND TENIENTE SUPPLY BITES
snippet: New York and Shanghai copper prices surged on April 17 as LME industrial metals hit a record high. Ongoing setbacks at Grasberg (Indonesia), El Teniente (Chile), and Kamoa-Kakula (DRC) are tightening physical supply. LME copper stockpiles remain exceptionally low, raising short-squeeze risk. Copper is up nearly 30% year-over-year on AI datacenter, defence, and tariff-driven demand.

headline: OPINION: GOLD MINERS FINALLY OUTPERFORMING THE METAL — GDX UP 108% YOY, FREE CASH FLOW RECORDS
snippet: Gold mining stocks are logging 108% year-over-year returns via GDX, now meaningfully outperforming bullion after years of lag. Analyst Jesse Colombo argues the structural re-rating has further to run: miners are generating record free cash flow at costs well below $2,000/oz while trading at valuations that still look cheap relative to the metal. A must-read for any investor choosing between GLD and GDX.

headline: $2.2B DEAL: G MINING VENTURES ACQUIRES G2 GOLDFIELDS, CREATING GUYANA GOLD HUB
snippet: G Mining Ventures (TSX: GMIN) agreed on April 9 to acquire G2 Goldfields in an all-share deal valued at approximately US$2.2 billion (C$3 billion), combining adjacent gold deposits in Guyana to create one of the largest, lowest-cost gold operations in the Americas. Deal expected to close Q2 2026. Benzinga analysis examines why the transaction is drawing attention to junior-tier consolidation in the current gold price environment.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD & SILVER FINDS 10 NEW HIGH-GRADE VEINS AT GALENA — 4,896 G/T SILVER INTERCEPT
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) has discovered ten new high-grade silver-copper-antimony and silver-lead veins at its Galena Complex in Idaho, including a 1.3-metre intercept grading 4,896 g/t silver and 3.95% copper. The company is launching its largest-ever drill program — 64,000 metres across Galena and the Cosalá operation in Mexico in 2026. High-grade silver in an established Idaho silver district at current silver prices.

headline: NEXGEN ENERGY COMMITS TO BUILD ROOK I THIS SUMMER — NORTH AMERICA'S LARGEST URANIUM MINE
snippet: NexGen Energy (TSX: NXE) has made its Final Investment Decision and will commence construction of the Rook I uranium mine in northern Saskatchewan this summer, following a Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission construction licence. At nearly 30 million pounds U3O8/year peak output, Rook I will be North America's largest uranium mine by output — ahead of Cameco's McArthur River and Cigar Lake operations.

headline: CORE LITHIUM AWARDS A$50M CONTRACT FOR FINNISS RESTART — MAY ORE EXTRACTION TARGET SET
snippet: Core Lithium (ASX: CXO) has awarded a A$50 million mining contract to NRW Holdings for surface mining at its Grants deposit, with ore extraction targeted to begin May 2026 and first spodumene concentrate shipments by late 2026. The Finniss restart — backed by A$290 million in new funding including a Glencore convertible note — signals that Australian lithium producers are re-entering the market as prices recover 78% from December lows.
